title: Drive Zephyr Meshtastic codec tests from canonical vectors
description: Why this exists: the Zephyr meshtastic_codec ztest currently mirrors selected bytes from test/vectors/meshtastic_app_compat.json, but it does not consume the canonical vector corpus directly. What needs to be done: add a generator or build-time conversion so Zephyr C tests are produced from the JSON vectors, including supported/unsupported cases and BLE prefix rejection where applicable, without dynamic allocation. Acceptance criteria: generated C fixture or embedded data is reproducible from the JSON vector file; native_sim ztest covers all codec-applicable vectors; Python and Zephyr remain in sync when vectors change.
notes: 2026-06-30 completed: added lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec build-time generator gen_vectors.py consuming test/vectors/meshtastic_app_compat.json and emitting meshtastic_vectors.h. Ztest asserts source vector count 14 and codec-applicable vector count 12, covers heartbeat, want_config_id, packet views, BLE stream-prefix rejection, oversized ToRadio rejection, queueStatus, FromRadio packet vectors, malformed/truncated/overflow varints, oversized field key, fixed64 skip, oneof last-wins, no-partial-write oversize encode, and sync response wrappers. EC2 post-fix Twister native_sim PASS: 1 scenario / 11 cases.
close_reason: Completed: canonical JSON-driven Zephyr meshtastic_codec vector harness implemented and native_sim Twister passed.
